Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Monuses and Heaps 38 upvotes, 3 comments
  2. The IBM scientist who rewrote the rules of information just won a Turing Award 43 upvotes, 4 comments
  3. Project Nomad – Knowledge That Never Goes Offline 213 upvotes, 41 comments
  4. Building an FPGA 3dfx Voodoo with Modern RTL Tools 107 upvotes, 19 comments
  5. My first patch to the Linux kernel 185 upvotes, 38 comments
  6. 25 Years of Eggs 184 upvotes, 54 comments
  7. A review of dice that came with the white castle 90 upvotes, 25 comments
  8. A Fuzzer for the Toy Optimizer 28 upvotes, 5 comments
  9. HopTab – Open source macOS app switcher and tiler that replaces Cmd+Tab 98 upvotes, 30 comments
  10. Flash-MoE: Running a 397B Parameter Model on a Laptop 219 upvotes, 83 comments
  11. MAUI Is Coming to Linux 45 upvotes, 12 comments
  12. More common mistakes to avoid when creating system architecture diagrams 92 upvotes, 35 comments
  13. The Future of Version Control 151 upvotes, 70 comments
  14. The three pillars of JavaScript bloat 422 upvotes, 246 comments
  15. Bored of eating your own dogfood? Try smelling your own farts 264 upvotes, 162 comments
  16. Node.js worker threads are problematic, but they work great for us 47 upvotes, 28 comments
  17. Cloudflare flags archive.today as "C&C/Botnet"; no longer resolves via 1.1.1.2 283 upvotes, 226 comments
  18. Brute-forcing my algorithmic ignorance with an LLM in 7 days 71 upvotes, 47 comments
  19. Why Lab Coats Turned White 46 upvotes, 30 comments
  20. Reports of code's death are greatly exaggerated 65 upvotes, 46 comments
  21. Why I love NixOS 54 upvotes, 41 comments
  22. Windows native app development is a mess 177 upvotes, 167 comments
  23. Learnings from training a font recognition model from scratch 5 upvotes, 1 comments
  24. How We Synchronized Editing for Rec Room's Multiplayer Scripting System 18 upvotes, 12 comments
  25. Five Years of Running a Systems Reading Group at Microsoft 4 upvotes, 1 comments
  26. A case against currying 62 upvotes, 78 comments
  27. Introducing DoorDash Tasks 18 upvotes, 18 comments
  28. Show HN: Revise – An AI Editor for Documents 25 upvotes, 28 comments
  29. Ask HN: AI productivity gains – do you fire devs or build better products? 61 upvotes, 92 comments
  30. Zero ZGC4: A Better Graphing Calculator for School and Beyond 7 upvotes, 8 comments

Last generated 2:30am, 23 March 2026. Source here.
